channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Critical Langflow Vulnerability Exploited Hours After Public Disclosure
item.description: Because attacker-supplied flow data is used in public flows, the bug leads to unauthenticated remote code execution. The post Critical Langflow Vulnerability Exploited Hours After Public Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Aisuru and Kimwolf DDoS Botnets Disrupted in International Operation
item.description: The lesser-known JackSkid and Mossad botnets have also been targeted in the operation. The post Aisuru and Kimwolf DDoS Botnets Disrupted in International Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Every Lab serious enough about Developers has bought their own Devtools
item.description: OpenAI buys Astral, Anthropic buys Bun, Google DeepMind bought the Antigravity team.

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad as part of a court-authorized law enforcement operation. The effort also saw authorities...

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword. These attacks employ malicious web content to target out-of-date versions of iOS, triggering an...

item.title: LibTV Launches: The First Professional Video Creation Platform for Both Humans and AI Agents
item.description: LibTV launches as the first professional video creation platform designed for both human creators and AI agents, enabling seamless, end-to-end video production.

item.title: AISTALAND Debuts First Model: Next-Generation Smart Wagon GT7
item.description: AISTALAND unveils its first smart wagon GT7, blending AI-driven intelligence with high-end design and advanced automotive technologies.

item.title: ModelBest Launches EdgeClaw Box: Secure, Ready-to-Use AI Agent Hardware
item.description: ModelBest launches EdgeClaw Box, a secure, out-of-the-box AI agent system that keeps sensitive data local while supporting cloud and edge collaboration.

item.title: Alibaba Unveils “Token Strategy,” Targets $100 Billion in Annual Cloud & AI Revenue Within Five Years
item.description: Alibaba is betting on a sweeping shift to the Token-driven AI economy, aiming to transform its cloud business into a $100 billion engine and redefine how markets value the company.

item.title: ADASPACE Achieves World’s First OpenClaw-Powered Space Computing Control of Ground Robots
item.description: A groundbreaking experiment by ADASPACE demonstrates, for the first time, how space-based AI computing can directly power and control robots on Earth through natural language.

item.title: DEAF: A Benchmark for Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness in Audio Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.18048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent Audio Multimodal Large Language Models (Audio MLLMs) demonstrate impressive performance on speech benchmarks, yet it remains unclear whether these models genuinely process acoustic signals or rely on text-based semantic inference. To...

item.title: Continually self-improving AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.18073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language model-based AI systems are remarkably powerful, yet their capabilities remain fundamentally capped by their human creators in three key ways. First, although a model's weights can be updated via fine-tuning, acquiring new knowledge...

item.title: Multi-Trait Subspace Steering to Reveal the Dark Side of Human-AI Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.18085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent incidents have highlighted alarming cases where human-AI interactions led to negative psychological outcomes, including mental health crises and even user harm. As LLMs serve as sources of guidance, emotional support, and even informal therapy,...

item.title: Adaptive Domain Models: Bayesian Evolution, Warm Rotation, and Principled Training for Geometric and Neuromorphic AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.18104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prevailing AI training infrastructure assumes reverse-mode automatic differentiation over IEEE-754 arithmetic. The memory overhead of training relative to inference, optimizer complexity, and structural degradation of geometric properties through...

item.title: Don't Vibe Code, Do Skele-Code: Interactive No-Code Notebooks for Subject Matter Experts to Build Lower-Cost Agentic Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2603.18122v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface for building workflows with AI agents, designed especially for less or non-technical users. It supports incremental, interactive notebook-style development, and each step is converted to code...

item.title: Frayed RoPE and Long Inputs: A Geometric Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.18017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE) is a widely adopted technique for encoding position in language models, which, while effective, causes performance breakdown when input length exceeds training length. Prior analyses assert (rightly) that long inputs...

item.title: Engineering Verifiable Modularity in Transformers via Per-Layer Supervision
item.description: arXiv:2603.18029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers resist surgical control. Ablating an attention head identified as critical for capitalization produces minimal behavioral change because distributed redundancy compensates for damage. This Hydra effect renders interpretability illusory:...

item.title: InfoMamba: An Attention-Free Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Model
item.description: arXiv:2603.18031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Balancing fine-grained local modeling with long-range dependency capture under computational constraints remains a central challenge in sequence modeling. While Transformers provide strong token mixing, they suffer from quadratic complexity, whereas...

item.title: Towards Differentiating Between Failures and Domain Shifts in Industrial Data Streams
item.description: arXiv:2603.18032v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anomaly and failure detection methods are crucial in identifying deviations from normal system operational conditions, which allows for actions to be taken in advance, usually preventing more serious damages. Long-lasting deviations indicate failures,...

item.title: Taming Epilepsy: Mean Field Control of Whole-Brain Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2603.18035v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Controlling the high-dimensional neural dynamics during epileptic seizures remains a significant challenge due to the nonlinear characteristics and complex connectivity of the brain. In this paper, we propose a novel framework, namely...

item.title: Do Large Language Models Possess a Theory of Mind? A Comparative Evaluation Using the Strange Stories Paradigm
item.description: arXiv:2603.18007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The study explores whether current Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit Theory of Mind (ToM) capabilities -- specifically, the ability to infer others' beliefs, intentions, and emotions from text. Given that LLMs are trained on language data without...

item.title: TherapyGym: Evaluating and Aligning Clinical Fidelity and Safety in Therapy Chatbots
item.description: arXiv:2603.18008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for mental-health support; yet prevailing evaluation methods--fluency metrics, preference tests, and generic dialogue benchmarks--fail to capture the clinically critical dimensions of psychotherapy....

item.title: How Confident Is the First Token? An Uncertainty-Calibrated Prompt Optimization Framework for Large Language Model Classification and Understanding
item.description: arXiv:2603.18009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) in natural language processing, prompt engineering and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) have become mainstream to enhance LLMs' performance on complex tasks. However, LLMs generate...

item.title: Agentic Framework for Political Biography Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2603.18010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The production of large-scale political datasets typically demands extracting structured facts from vast piles of unstructured documents or web sources, a task that traditionally relies on expensive human experts and remains prohibitively difficult to...

item.title: Controllable Evidence Selection in Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering via Deterministic Utility Gating
item.description: arXiv:2603.18011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many modern AI question-answering systems convert text into vectors and retrieve the closest matches to a user question. While effective for topical similarity, similarity scores alone do not explain why some retrieved text can serve as evidence while...

item.title: Disruption with Copilot Coding Agent Sessions
item.description: Mar 20, 01:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 20, 01:26 UTCUpdate - We are rolling out our mitigation and are seeing recovery.Mar 20,...

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets --...

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitiously harvest sensitive information from infected computers and transmit it to a Cobra DocGuard...

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 34 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 34 vulnerable drivers. EDR killer programs have been a common presence in ransomware intrusions as they offer a...

item.title: Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management
item.description: The company will invest in R&D, product expansion across AI frameworks, and in scaling go-to-market and sales efforts. The post Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding
item.description: The company’s endpoint security platform monitors behavior and verifies user intent to stop cyberattacks in real time. The post 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys
item.description: Latest ScreenConnect version adds encrypted storage and management to prevent unauthorized access to machine keys. The post Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Git operations for users in the west coast are experiencing an increase in latency
item.description: Mar 19, 16:25 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Git Operations

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 19, 16:16 UTCResolved - This incident started at 8:53 PT / 15:53 UTC and the errors have now returned to baseline since 9:14 PT / 16:14 UTC.Mar 19, 15:59 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Cloudflare Pages API is seeing increased error rate.
item.description: Mar 19, 15:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 19, 15:54 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare Pages API is seeing increased error rate. This affects the Workers & Pages section in the Cloudflare Dashboard, as well as Workers Builds. Deployed Workers and Pages projects are unaffected

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 19, 15:46 UTCResolved - As of 8:42 PT / 15:42 UTC the error rate has now returned to baseline.Mar 19, 15:37 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating elevated errors that started at 8:23 PT / 15:23 UTC.

item.title: Issues with Copilot Coding Agent
item.description: Mar 19, 14:32 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 19, 14:06 UTCUpdate - Copilot is operating normally.Mar 19, 14:02 UTCUpdate - We are...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn’t work anymore but still do. Some of it looks simple, almost sloppy, until you see how well it lands. Other...

item.title: **Introducing SPEED-Bench: A Unified and Diverse Benchmark for Speculative Decoding**
The Download: Quantum computing for health, and why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can solve health care problems  In a laboratory on the outskirts of Oxford, a quantum...

item.title: How to secure your online meetings
item.description: Advice for small and medium-sized organisations on choosing, setting up and using online meeting services safely.

item.title: Increased HTTP 530 Errors in Atlanta (ATL)
item.description: Mar 19, 11:36 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased rate of HTTP 530 errors specifically affecting traffic routed through our Atlanta (ATL) data center. We are working to identify the root cause and will provide updates as they become available.

item.title: A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can solve health care problems
item.description: I’m standing in front of a quantum computer built out of atoms and light at the UK’s National Quantum Computing Centre on the outskirts of Oxford. On a laboratory table, a complex matrix of mirrors and lenses surrounds a Rubik’s Cube–size cell where 100 cesium atoms are suspended in grid formation...

item.title: Why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: The prospect of making trash useful is always fascinating to me. Whether it’s used batteries, solar panels, or spent nuclear fuel, getting use out of something destined for disposal sounds like a win all around. In nuclear energy, figuring out what to do with waste has always been a challenge,...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Mumbai, India
item.description: Mar 19, 08:43 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 19, 08:37 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating network performance issues that may impact a subset of users in Mumbai, India. Some users may experience increased latency or intermittent errors....

item.title: [AINews] MiniMax 2.7: GLM-5 at 1/3 cost SOTA Open Model
item.description: congrats MiniMax!!

item.title: Disruption with Copilot Coding Agent sessions
item.description: Mar 19, 02:25 UTCUpdate - We are seeing widespread issues starting and viewing Copilot Agent sessions. We have a hypothesis for the cause and are working on remediation.Mar 19, 02:05 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 19, 01:44 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 19, 01:43 UTCUpdate - We are seeing recovery in git operations for customers on the...

item.title: Elevated errors across surfaces
item.description: Mar 19, 01:38 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 19, 01:21 UTCMonitoring - From 23:59–00:30 UTC, users experienced elevated errors when trying to authenticate across various Claude surfaces including Claude.ai and Claude Code. This issue has been resolved, and we are monitoring...

item.title: Performance degradation in one of the Cloudflare Los Angeles POP
item.description: Mar 18, 23:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 18, 21:29 UTCMonitoring - We have mitigated an issue in one of the Cloudflare Los Angeles POPs causing performance degradation between 20:32 to 20:53 UTC
